volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 1 - Section 155)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: ὦ κοινὸν αὐτάδελφον Ἰσμήνης κάρα ,
sections.translation: Ismene , my sister , true child of my own mother , do you know any evil out of all the evils bequeathed by Oedipus that Zeus will not fulfil for the two of us in our lifetime ? There is nothing — no pain , no ruin ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: οὔτʼ αἰσχρὸν οὔτʼ ἄτιμόν ἐσθʼ , ὁποῖον οὐ
sections.translation: no shame , nor dishonor — that I have not seen in your sufferings and mine . And now what is this new edict that they say the general has just decreed to all the city ? Do you know anything ? Have you heard ? Or does it escape you that

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: πρὸς τοὺς φίλους στείχοντα τῶν ἐχθρῶν κακά ;
sections.translation: evils from our enemies are on the march against our friends ?

sections.label: Section 11
sections.original: ἐμοὶ μὲν οὐδεὶς μῦθος , Ἀντιγόνη , φίλων
sections.translation: To me no word of our friends , Antigone , either bringing joy or bringing pain has come since we two were robbed of our two brothers who died in one day by a double blow .

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ φροῦδός ἐστιν Ἀργείων στρατὸς
sections.translation: And since the Argive army has fled during this night , I have learned nothing further , whether better fortune is mine , or further ruin .

sections.label: Section 18
sections.original: ᾔδη καλῶς , καί σʼ ἐκτὸς αὐλείων πυλῶν
sections.translation: I knew it well , so I was trying to bring you outside the courtyard gates to this end , that you alone might hear .

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστι ; δηλοῖς γάρ τι καλχαίνουσʼ ἔπος .
sections.translation: Hear what ? It is clear that you are brooding on some dark news .

sections.label: Section 21
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ τάφου νῷν τὼ κασιγνήτω Κρέων
sections.translation: Why not ? Has not Creon destined our brothers , the one to honored burial , the other to unburied shame ? Eteocles , they say , with due observance of right and custom , he has laid in the earth

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: ἔκρυψε τοῖς ἔνερθεν ἔντιμον νεκροῖς ·
sections.translation: for his honor among the dead below . As for the poor corpse of Polyneices , however , they say that an edict has been published to the townsmen that no one shall bury him or mourn him , but instead leave him unwept , unentombed , for the birds a pleasing store

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: θησαυρὸν εἰσορῶσι πρὸς χάριν βορᾶς .
sections.translation: as they look to satisfy their hunger . Such , it is said , is the edict that the good Creon has laid down for you and for me — yes , for me — and it is said that he is coming here to proclaim it for the certain knowledge of those who do not already know . They say that he does not conduct this business lightly ,

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: οὐχ ὡς παρʼ οὐδέν , ἀλλʼ ὃς ἂν τούτων τι δρᾷ ,
sections.translation: but whoever performs any of these rites , for him the fate appointed is death by public stoning among the entire city . This is how things stand for you , and so you will soon show your nature , whether you are noble-minded , or the corrupt daughter of a noble line .

sections.label: Section 39
sections.original: τί δʼ , ὦ ταλαῖφρον , εἰ τάδʼ ἐν τούτοις , ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Poor sister , if things have come to this , what would I

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: λύουσʼ ἂν ἢ ʼφάπτουσα προσθείμην πλέον ;
sections.translation: profit by loosening or tightening this knot ?

sections.label: Section 41
sections.original: εἰ ξυμπονήσεις καὶ ξυνεργάσει σκόπει .
sections.translation: Consider whether you will share the toil and the task .

sections.label: Section 42
sections.original: ποῖόν τι κινδύνευμα ; ποῦ γνώμης ποτʼ εἶ ;
sections.translation: What are you hazarding ? What do you intend ?

sections.label: Section 43
sections.original: εἰ τὸν νεκρὸν ξὺν τῇδε κουφιεῖς χερί .
sections.translation: Will you join your hand to mine in order to lift his corpse ?

sections.label: Section 44
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ νοεῖς θάπτειν σφʼ , ἀπόρρητον πόλει ;
sections.translation: You plan to bury him — when it is forbidden to the city ?

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: τὸν γοῦν ἐμὸν καὶ τὸν σόν , ἢν σὺ μὴ θέλῃς
sections.translation: Yes , he is my brother , and yours too , even if you wish it otherwise . I will never be convicted of betraying him .

sections.label: Section 47
sections.original: ὦ σχετλία , Κρέοντος ἀντειρηκότος ;
sections.translation: Hard girl ! Even when Creon has forbidden it ?

sections.label: Section 48
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδὲν αὐτῷ τῶν ἐμῶν μʼ εἴργειν μέτα .
sections.translation: No , he has no right to keep me from my own .

sections.label: Section 49
sections.original: οἴμοι . φρόνησον , ὦ κασιγνήτη , πατὴρ
sections.translation: Ah , no ! Think , sister , how our father

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: ὡς νῷν ἀπεχθὴς δυσκλεής τʼ ἀπώλετο ,
sections.translation: perished in hatred and infamy , when , because of the crimes that he himself detected , he smashed both his eyes with self-blinding hand ; then his mother-wife , two names in one , with a twisted noose destroyed her life ;

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: τρίτον δʼ ἀδελφὼ δύο μίαν καθʼ ἡμέραν
sections.translation: lastly , our two brothers in a single day , both unhappy murderers of their own flesh and blood , worked with mutual hands their common doom . And now we , in turn — we two who have been left all alone — consider how much more miserably we will be destroyed , if in defiance of the law

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: ψῆφον τυράννων ἢ κράτη παρέξιμεν .
sections.translation: we transgress against an autocrat ’ s decree or his powers . No , we must remember , first , that ours is a woman ’ s nature , and accordingly not suited to battles against men ; and next , that we are ruled by the more powerful , so that we must obey in these things and in things even more stinging .

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν οὖν αἰτοῦσα τοὺς ὑπὸ χθονὸς
sections.translation: I , therefore , will ask those below for pardon , since I am forced to this , and will obey those who have come to authority . It is foolish to do what is fruitless .

sections.label: Section 69
sections.original: οὔτʼ ἂν κελεύσαιμʼ οὔτʼ ἄν , εἰ θέλοις ἔτι
sections.translation: I would not encourage you — no , nor , even if you were willing later ,

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: πράσσειν , ἐμοῦ γʼ ἂν ἡδέως δρῴης μέτα .
sections.translation: would I welcome you as my partner in this action . No , be the sort that pleases you . I will bury him — it would honor me to die while doing that . I shall rest with him , loved one with loved one , a pious criminal . For the time is greater

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ὃν δεῖ μʼ ἀρέσκειν τοῖς κάτω τῶν ἐνθάδε .
sections.translation: that I must serve the dead than the living , since in that world I will rest forever . But if you so choose , continue to dishonor what the gods in honor have established .

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν οὐκ ἄτιμα ποιοῦμαι , τὸ δὲ
sections.translation: I do them no dishonor . But to act in violation of the citizens ’ will — of that I am by nature incapable .

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: σὺ μὲν τάδʼ ἂν προὔχοιʼ · ἐγὼ δὲ δὴ τάφον
sections.translation: You can make that your pretext ! Regardless , I will go now to heap a tomb over the brother I love .

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: οἴμοι ταλαίνης , ὡς ὑπερδέδοικά σου .
sections.translation: Oh no , unhappy sister ! I fear for you !

sections.label: Section 83
sections.original: μὴ ʼμοῦ προτάρβει · τὸν σὸν ἐξόρθου πότμον .
sections.translation: Do not tremble for me . Straighten out your own destiny .

sections.label: Section 84
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὖν προμηνύσῃς γε τοῦτο μηδενὶ
sections.translation: Then at least disclose the deed to no one before you do it .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: τοὔργον , κρυφῇ δὲ κεῦθε , σὺν δʼ αὔτως ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Conceal it , instead , in secrecy — and so , too , will I .

sections.label: Section 86
sections.original: οἴμοι , καταύδα · πολλὸν ἐχθίων ἔσει
sections.translation: Go on ! Denounce it ! You will be far more hated for your silence , if you fail to proclaim these things to everyone .

sections.label: Section 88
sections.original: θερμὴν ἐπὶ ψυχροῖσι καρδίαν ἔχεις .
sections.translation: You have a hot heart for chilling deeds .

sections.label: Section 89
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἶδʼ ἀρέσκουσʼ οἷς μάλισθʼ ἁδεῖν με χρή .
sections.translation: I know that I please those whom I am most bound to please .

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: εἰ καὶ δυνήσει γʼ · ἀλλʼ ἀμηχάνων ἐρᾷς .
sections.translation: Yes , if you will also have the power . But you crave the impossible .

sections.label: Section 91
sections.original: οὐκοῦν , ὅταν δὴ μὴ σθένω , πεπαύσομαι .
sections.translation: Why then , when my strength fails , I will have finished .

sections.label: Section 92
sections.original: ἀρχὴν δὲ θηρᾶν οὐ πρέπει τἀμήχανα .
sections.translation: An impossible hunt should not be tried in the first place .

sections.label: Section 93
sections.original: εἰ ταῦτα λέξεις , ἐχθαρεῖ μὲν ἐξ ἐμοῦ ,
sections.translation: If you mean that , you will have my hatred , and you will be subject to punishment as the enemy of the dead .

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔα με καὶ τὴν ἐξ ἐμοῦ δυσβουλίαν
sections.translation: But leave me and the foolish plan I have authored to suffer this terrible thing , for I will not suffer anything so terrible that my death will lack honor .

sections.label: Section 98
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ δοκεῖ σοι , στεῖχε · τοῦτο δʼ ἴσθʼ ὅτι
sections.translation: Go , then , if you so decide . And of this be sure : though your path is foolish , to your loved ones your love is straight and true . Exit Antigone on the spectators ’ left . Ismene exits into the palace .

sections.label: Section 1.100
sections.original: ἀκτὶς ἀελίου , τὸ κάλλιστον ἑπταπύλῳ φανὲν
sections.translation: Shaft of the sun , fairest light of all that have dawned on Thebes of the seven gates , you have shone forth at last , eye of golden day , advancing over Dirce ’ s streams !

sections.label: Section 1.105
sections.original: Διρκαίων ὑπὲρ ῥεέθρων μολοῦσα ,
sections.translation: You have goaded with a sharper bit the warrior of the white shield , who came from Argos in full armor , driving him to headlong retreat .

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: ὃς ἐφʼ ἡμετέρᾳ γᾷ Πολυνείκους
sections.translation: He set out against our land because of the strife-filled claims of Polyneices , and like a screaming eagle he flew over into our land , covered by his snow-white wing ,

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: πολλῶν μεθʼ ὅπλων
sections.translation: with a mass of weapons and crested helmets .

sections.label: Section 1.117
sections.original: στὰς δʼ ὑπὲρ μελάθρων φονώσαισιν ἀμφιχανὼν κύκλῳ
sections.translation: He paused above our dwellings ; he gaped around our sevenfold portals with spears thirsting for blood ; but he left

sections.label: Section 1.120
sections.original: αἱμάτων γένυσιν πλησθῆναί τε καὶ στεφάνωμα πύργων
sections.translation: before his jaws were ever glutted with our gore , or before the Fire-god ’ s pine-fed flame had seized our crown of towers .

sections.label: Section 1.125
sections.original: πάταγος Ἄρεος , ἀντιπάλῳ δυσχείρωμα δράκοντος .
sections.translation: So fierce was the crash of battle swelling about his back , a match too hard to win for the rival of the dragon .

sections.label: Section 127
sections.original: Ζεὺς γὰρ μεγάλης γλώσσης κόμπους
sections.translation: For Zeus detests above all the boasts of a proud tongue . And when he saw them advancing in a swollen flood ,

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: χρυσοῦ καναχῆς ὑπεροπλίαις ,
sections.translation: arrogant their clanging gold , he dashed with brandished fire one who was already starting to shout victory when he had reached our ramparts .

sections.label: Section 2.134
sections.original: ἀντιτύπᾳ δʼ ἐπὶ γᾷ πέσε τανταλωθεὶς
sections.translation: Staggered , he fell to the earth with a crash ,

sections.label: Section 2.135
sections.original: πυρφόρος , ὃς τότε μαινομένᾳ ξὺν ὁρμᾷ
sections.translation: torch in hand , a man possessed by the frenzy of the mad attack , who just now was raging against us with the blasts of his tempestuous hate . But his threats did not fare as he had hoped , and to the other enemies mighty Ares dispensed each their own dooms with hard blows ,

sections.label: Section 2.140
sections.original: δεξιόσειρος .
sections.translation: Ares , our mighty ally at the turning-point .

sections.label: Section 141
sections.original: ἑπτὰ λοχαγοὶ γὰρ ἐφʼ ἑπτὰ πύλαις
sections.translation: For the seven captains , stationed against an equal number at the seven gates , left behind their brazen arms in tribute to Zeus the turner of battle — all but the accursed pair who , born of one father and one

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: μητρός τε μιᾶς φύντε καθʼ αὑτοῖν
sections.translation: mother , set against each other their spears , both victorious , and who now share in a common death .

sections.label: Section 2.148
sections.original: ἀλλὰ γὰρ ἁ μεγαλώνυμος ἦλθε Νίκα
sections.translation: But since Victory whose name is glory has come to us , smiling in joy equal to the joy of chariot-rich Thebes ,

sections.label: Section 2.150
sections.original: ἐκ μὲν δὴ πολέμων
sections.translation: let us make for ourselves forgetfulness after the recent wars , and visit all the temples of the gods with night-long dance and song . And may Bacchus , who shakes the earth of Thebes , rule our dancing !

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὅδε γὰρ δὴ βασιλεὺς χώρας ,
sections.translation: But look , the king of the land is coming here , Creon , the son of Menoeceus , our new ruler in accordance with the new circumstances fated by the gods . What policy is he setting in motion ,
